mentions.note: Excellent company Cramer follows closely, but he owns Nvidia instead of doubling up on the same AI chip exposure.
Expects a monster quarter that could help shake off selling pressure in partner Nvidia's stock.
Reported one of strongest quarters ever with insatiable AI chip demand; spending $50B+ to keep up with orders; validates AI investment thesis and hyperscaler profitability.
Amazing semiconductor manufacturer; critical for Nvidia and others; excellent tech holding.
Partners with Nvidia on high-end chips; no bottleneck or shortage in their segment; critical to global AI infrastructure.
Semiconductor manufacturer; key partner to Nvidia; critical to high-end chip production.
Blowout quarter; guiding 52-56B capex for 2026 (up 27-37%) vs. <45B expected; management says supply-demand won't balance until 2028-2029; bullish for years of equipment orders.
Cramer likes it very much but recommends Nvidia instead as better entry point.
Monster quarter reported; CEO C.C. Wei signaled shift from generative AI query mode to agentic AI command-action mode—Nvidia supremacy moment.
Very good situation; stock went down on good quarter last week—buying opportunity; room to grow from current levels.
More business than can handle; Arm Holdings confirmed TSM supply bottleneck; add to position.
Chip manufacturer making AI chips; part of semiconductor layer of AI infrastructure.

Cramer: 'I think the world of it, I think it's going to go higher' but 'may not necessarily skyrocket.' Indispensable choke point in advanced manufacturing; underperformed Micron/Nvidia/AMD parabolic moves but room to run.
